steps.title: Identify the gap between existing frameworks and what matters
steps.content: Tavinor and Gaut explain games through narrative (fiction and cinema), but the passage defines the central aspect of gameplay as player agency—what absorbs players when narrative becomes irrelevant.

steps.title: Find what Nguyen's critique must conclude about these frameworks
steps.content: D identifies that Tavinor and Gaut subsume games under categories (fiction, cinema) that don't address player agency, the feature integral to gaming. A, B, and C miss this structural problem, instead overstating the frameworks' worth or redirecting the critique elsewhere. That matches choice D — D is correct.
